dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the council voted last night. If we can't find a solution by the deadline, they're going to sacrifice {quest_stakes_the} to save the rest.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: There are people trapped by {quest_threat_the}. The council says it's a few lives against many. They're not wrong about the math, but they might be wrong about the deadline. {quest_origin} might hold answers, but nobody has dared go there since the {enemy_type_plural} moved in. Someone needs to {quest_action} before {quest_time_pressure}.
dialogueChunks.outro: I can't just watch them be abandoned. Not while there's still a chance.

dialogueChunks.intro: The {enemy_type_plural} sent {quest_focus_the} this morning, {player_name}. They have our people. They want our survival stores in exchange. All of them.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: If we surrender the stores, the settlement starves. If we refuse, families lose someone they love. The {enemy_type_plural} chose their hostages carefully: people the settlement can't bear to lose. Every option on the table costs lives.
dialogueChunks.outro: There might be a third option. {quest_origin} is where they're being held. If someone could {quest_action} before the deadline, we wouldn't have to choose which of our people to sacrifice. {quest_item} {location_hint} could give us the edge we need.

dialogueChunks.intro: The commander wants to destroy {quest_focus_the}. It's the only thing between {quest_threat_the} and the settlement, {player_name}. But there are still people on the other side.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: If {quest_focus_the} stands, the {enemy_type_plural} pour through and everyone dies. If we destroy it now, the people on the far side are cut off with no escape. The commander is waiting for an answer. I don't have one.
dialogueChunks.outro: If there's another way through, somewhere beyond {quest_focus_the}, those people have a chance. Someone needs to {quest_action} before {quest_time_pressure}. Once the order is given, there's no going back.
